description: Fix tools in Supabase and Cloudflare that bypass their plugin's own api() function: supabase/generate-types.ts uses raw fetch() and cloudflare/graphql-query.ts bypasses cloudflareApi(). Also fix inconsistent encodeURIComponent in Sentry.

userStories.title: Fix Supabase generate-types.ts to use plugin api()
userStories.description: supabase/src/tools/generate-types.ts uses raw fetch() with duplicated auth extraction instead of the plugin's own api() function. Migrate to use the plugin's existing API caller.
acceptanceCriteria: generate-types.ts uses the plugin's api() or fetchFromPage from SDK instead of raw fetch()
Auth extraction is not duplicated — uses the existing getAuth() from supabase-api.ts
Error handling is consistent with other Supabase tools
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/supabase/src/tools/generate-types.ts

Context:
This tool calls the Supabase REST endpoint to get TypeScript type definitions. It currently uses raw `fetch()` with manual auth token extraction, duplicating the logic in supabase-api.ts. The endpoint returns text/plain content (TypeScript source), not JSON.

The fix should either:
1. Use `fetchText()` from the SDK with the auth token from `getAuth()` in supabase-api.ts
2. Or add a text-returning variant to the plugin's API caller

Since the endpoint returns text and the existing `api()` returns JSON, the cleanest approach is to use `fetchText` from the SDK directly, reusing the auth from `supabase-api.ts`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change supabase-api.ts
- The endpoint returns text/plain, not JSON — do NOT use fetchJSON

userStories.title: Fix Cloudflare graphql-query.ts to use cloudflareApi()
userStories.description: cloudflare/src/tools/graphql-query.ts bypasses cloudflareApi() and uses raw fetch() for the GraphQL endpoint. Migrate to use the plugin's existing API caller.
acceptanceCriteria: graphql-query.ts uses cloudflareApi() or fetchFromPage from SDK instead of raw fetch()
Error handling is consistent with other Cloudflare tools
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/cloudflare/src/tools/graphql-query.ts

Context:
The GraphQL query tool calls https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/graphql which is a different base URL than what cloudflareApi() uses. It currently uses raw fetch() with manual header construction.

The fix should use `fetchFromPage` from the SDK with the auth token from `getAuth()` in cloudflare-api.ts. The cloudflareApi() function uses a configurable base URL pattern, so if it can accept the GraphQL endpoint URL directly, use it. Otherwise, use `postJSON` from the SDK with the auth headers from the existing auth functions.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change cloudflare-api.ts unless necessary to expose auth headers
- The GraphQL endpoint is at api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/graphql — verify the base URL is correct

userStories.title: Fix inconsistent encodeURIComponent in Sentry
userStories.description: Some Sentry tools encode project_slug in URLs while others don't, despite all interpolating it the same way. Standardize to always encode.
acceptanceCriteria: All Sentry tools that interpolate project_slug into URLs use encodeURIComponent
Consistent encoding across list-project-environments.ts, get-project.ts, get-project-keys.ts
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/sentry/src/tools/list-project-environments.ts (already encodes)
- plugins/sentry/src/tools/get-project.ts (does NOT encode — add encodeURIComponent)
- plugins/sentry/src/tools/get-project-keys.ts (does NOT encode — add encodeURIComponent)

The fix is simple: wrap `params.project_slug` with `encodeURIComponent()` in the API call URL template for get-project.ts and get-project-keys.ts.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other tools
- Do NOT change the API function or schemas
